Title: Investigation of the mechanisms involved in the biocontrol activities of natural products from a marine soil bacterium against rice blast

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: phenazine core intermediates leading to PCA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes a core phenazine biosynthetic cluster required for production of phenazine intermediates leading to phenazine-1-carboxylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal activity against Pyricularia oryzae and other plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of phz1 supports biosynthesis of PCA/PCN in strain R64
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCR detection; gel electrophoresis; HPLC; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering increased phenazine production in microbial biopesticide development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phz1 was detected in Pseudomonas aeruginosa R64 by PCR. 2. Its presence supported the existence of a phenazine biosynthetic capacity in R64. 3. This supported accumulation of PCA/PCN in the fermentation extract linked to antifungal activity against rice blast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected as contextual support for metabolite identification; no direct gene manipulation or step-specific enzyme assay was performed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: phenazine core intermediates leading to PCA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes a second core phenazine biosynthetic cluster required for production of phenazine intermediates leading to phenazine-1-carboxylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal activity against Pyricularia oryzae and other plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of phz2 supports biosynthesis of PCA/PCN in strain R64
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCR detection; gel electrophoresis; HPLC; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering increased phenazine production in microbial biopesticide development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phz2 was detected in Pseudomonas aeruginosa R64 by PCR. 2. Its presence supported the existence of a phenazine biosynthetic capacity in R64. 3. This supported accumulation of PCA/PCN in the fermentation extract linked to antifungal activity against rice blast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected as contextual support for metabolite identification; no direct gene manipulation or step-specific enzyme assay was performed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Principal antifungal effector against Pyricularia oryzae in RFE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of phzH supports biosynthesis of PCN, the principal antifungal component in RFE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCR detection; gel electrophoresis; HPLC; LC-MS; comparative metabolite bioassays with PCN and PCA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to enhance PCN yield for biopesticide production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzH was detected in Pseudomonas aeruginosa R64 by PCR. 2. The paper states phzH is involved in conversion of PCA to PCN. 3. Because PCN showed stronger antifungal activity than PCA, phzH-supported PCN production is closely linked to the bioactivity of RFE.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the most metabolism-relevant gene in the paper, but still supported only by detection and metabolite inference rather than direct genetic perturbation in this study.
